steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts two things: the Nobel Prize going to “a single person” (like de Hevesy) versus recognition extending beyond that. The word “But” introduces the pivot, and “Instead” reinforces it by pointing toward collaboration. The blank must name the type of individual the passage is moving away from — the lone, unaccompanied kind that stands opposite to collaborative success.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — solitary: Means working or existing alone — the direct opposite of collaboration. “Recognition isn't restricted to solitary individuals” sets up the contrast perfectly: the prize also goes to those who work with others, not just isolated researchers. ✓
  • Choice B — coordinated: Means organized together. This describes collaborative individuals, not isolated ones — the opposite of what the contrast requires. ✗
  • Choice C — considerate: Means thoughtful of others. Unrelated to whether someone works alone or with others; does not create the lone-vs.-collaborative contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — coincidental: Means happening by chance. Irrelevant to the passage's discussion of individual versus collaborative achievement. ✗
That is the correct answer.
